perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Information expires the longer of three (3) years from the date set forth above or after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS is completed , unless the Parties agree in writing to a different term .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Information expires three (3) years from the date set forth above, regardless of the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS, unless the Parties agree in writing to a different term. However, any Confidential Information related to decommissioning activities will remain protected for five (5) years after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the expiration of the confidentiality period. The first sentence sets a clear expiration term of three years or the conclusion of decommissioning, whichever is longer. The added second sentence introduces a new, separate protection period of five years after decommissioning specifically for information related to those activities. This contradicts the initial expiration term and causes confusion over when confidentiality ends, creating potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Period . This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Information expires three (3) years from the date set forth above, regardless of the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS, unless the Parties agree in writing to a different term. However, any Confidential Information related to decommissioning activities will remain protected for five (5) years after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it initially sets an expiration term of three years from the date or the conclusion of decommissioning, whichever is longer, for Confidential Information. However, it then adds a contradictory statement that any Confidential Information related to decommissioning activities will remain protected for five years after the conclusion of decommissioning. This contradicts the initial expiration term and causes confusion, as the same information now has two different protection periods, creating potential disputes. Since the Contradicted Text and Change Text is the same, the contradictory text exists in the same section. Therefore, based on the Perturbation Explanation, it indicates that there's indeed a contradiction that needs a 'YES' score.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will return to the Disclosing Party , upon demand or in the event either Party ceases to be interested in pursuing the Purpose, all Confidential Information provided to Recipient, including all copies thereof which may have been made by or on behalf of Recipient, and Recipient shall destroy, or cause to be destroyed, all notes or memoranda or other stored information of any kind prepared by Recipient relating to the Confi dential Information or discussions generally.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will return to the Disclosing Party, only upon a written demand from the Disclosing Party, all Confidential Information provided to Recipient, including all copies thereof which may have been made by or on behalf of Recipient. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient is allowed to keep an archival copy of all documents for internal auditing purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the return and destruction of confidential information. The original clause states that the Recipient must return or destroy all Confidential Information when either party loses interest in the Purpose. The altered clause allows the Recipient to retain an archival copy of all documents for internal auditing, despite the original requirement to return or destroy all such information, leading to potential conflict over compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction. Recipient will return to the Disclosing Party, only upon a written demand from the Disclosing Party, all Confidential Information provided to Recipient, including all copies thereof which may have been made by or on behalf of Recipient. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient is allowed to keep an archival copy of all documents for internal auditing purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original requirement of returning or destroying all Confidential Information by adding an exception that allows the Recipient to retain an archival copy for internal auditing. This directly contradicts the initial instruction to return all copies. The 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the Changed Text, indicating that the contradiction exists within that specific section as a result of the modification. The modified text introduces an inconsistency by allowing the retention of confidential information under certain circumstances, while the original text required its complete return or destruction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement embodies the entire understanding between the P arties pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by both P arties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement embodies the entire understanding between the Parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made verbally with witnesses, but must be put in writing within 30 days of the verbal agreement
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation directly contradicts the integrated agreement clause, the clause originally mandates that all modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. the change allowing verbal agreements undermines this clause, creating uncertainty about the validity of modifications. This in-text contradiction could lead to disagreements about whether verbal agreements are enforceable and affects the terms should be enforced, and if they are, what is the importance of having the verbal agreement in writing within 30 days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement embodies the entire understanding between the Parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made verbally with witnesses, but must be put in writing within 30 days of the verbal agreement
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that modifications to the agreement can be made verbally with witnesses, which directly contradicts the original requirement for all modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties. The contradicted text shows that the clause about verbal agreements was added, showing there is a clear contradiction since the original entire agreement clause mandated written agreements. This creates a strong contradiction because the enforceability and validity of verbal agreements becomes unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing cont ained in this Agreement or in any discussions undertaken, or disclosures made pursuant hereto shall: (a ) be interpreted or relied upon, directly or indirectly, by either Part as a commitment or intent to engage in a business relationship, contract , or futur e dealings with the other Party; and (b ) prevent either Party from entering into similar discussions with unrelated third parties so long as such discussions do not violate the terms and conditions imposed by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement or in any discussions undertaken, or disclosures made pursuant hereto shall: (a ) be interpreted or relied upon, directly or indirectly, by either Part as a commitment or intent to engage in a business relationship, contract , or futur e dealings with the other Party, unless a formal agreement is drafted within 60 days of initial disclosure; and (b ) prevent either Party from entering into similar discussions with unrelated third parties so long as such discussions do not violate the terms and conditions imposed by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification contradicts the 'No Binding Commitment' clause by introducing a condition that implies a potential commitment. While the original clause states that nothing should be interpreted as a commitment, the change states that if a formal agreement is drafted within 60 days of initial disclosure, the discussions can be interpreted as intent to engage in a business relationship. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about whether the discussions can create a binding commitment under specific circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Binding Commitment. Nothing cont ained in this Agreement or in any discussions undertaken, or disclosures made pursuant hereto shall: (a ) be interpreted or relied upon, directly or indirectly, by either Part as a commitment or intent to engage in a business relationship, contract , or futur e dealings with the other Party, unless a formal agreement is drafted within 60 days of initial disclosure; and (b ) prevent either Party from entering into similar discussions with unrelated third parties so long as such discussions do not violate the terms and conditions imposed by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'No Binding Commitment' clause by adding an exception: if a formal agreement is drafted within 60 days, the discussions can be interpreted as intent to engage in a business relationship. The original text states nothing should be interpreted as a commitment, and now the changed text says it can be interpreted as a commitment under specific circumstances. Since the Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, it creates a direct in-text contradiction, falling under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
